rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ERNEST V. RHINE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Timely Serve Named Defendant Ronald Gagosian, And For Failure To Prosecute
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Respondent's Motion to Dismiss Will Contest for Failure to Prosecute is DENIED. Respondent concedes in footnote 1 in her responsive papers filed March 9, 2021 that the motion in the alternative for mandatory dismissal under CCP section 583.210 et seq for lack of service of summons and complaint within three years lacks merit, but contends that discretionary dismissal under CCP section 583.410 et seq remains appropriate. Not so. Respondent has not met her initial burden to show a failure to prosecute and prejudice suffered by Respondent as a result (see Cubit v. Ridgecrest Cmty. Hosp. (1987) 194 Cal. App. 3d 1552, 1566, and Marra v. Mission Foods Corp. (1993) 19 Cal. App. 4th 724, 727.) The Second Amended Petition to Contest the Validity of the Challenged First and Second Sets of Amendments to the Ernest V. Rhine Declaration of Trust, UDT April 9, 2000 on the Grounds of Undue Influence was filed September 13, 2018. It has been served on the appropriate parties. In its discretion, the court does not find that dismissal of the action is appropriate under the circumstances of the case. (see CCP sections 583.410, 583.130 and CRC 3.1342.) As of the March 22, 2021 probate calendar hearing, trial on The Second Amended Petition to Contest the Validity of the Challenged First and Second Sets of Amendments to the Ernest V. Rhine Declaration of Trust, UDT April 9, 2000 on the Grounds of Undue Influence has been set for trial on October 4, 2021. The court overrules all evidentiary objections, from both Respondents and Petitioner/Contestant. Specially, evidentiary objections 1-25 filed by Petitioner/Contestant on February 19, 2021 are overruled; and evidentiary objections 1-10 filed by Respondents on March 16, 2021 are overruled. Petitioner shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a hard-copy proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on March 23, 2021. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925#, participant ID 0#, and passcode 722809#. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES HAROLD ADAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/5/21. None of the parties has received notice of an AMENDED petition to probate a LOST WILL. (An amended notice of a petition is not the same as notice of an AMENDED PETITION.) Notice mailed on 3/10/21 was not at least 15 days before this hearing. Notice of the AMENDED PETITION requesting that the decedent's LOST WILL be admitted must be served on all interested parties at least 15 days before 5/5/21. Additionally, notice was mailed to Lorinda Ellsworth, Merton Michael Adams, and Rosanna Adams at addresses that differ from the ones shown in the supplement filed on 3/11/21. Item 8 still has not been supplemented to identify who the decedent's parents were and when they died. [LRSF 14.18 C.3.] What was Marilyn Hannah's relationship to the decedent and when did she die? If she is a relative, was she survived by issue? [LRSF 14.18 D.] Did the decedent's brother, Larry Adams, die without issue? Richard Adams and Barbara Adams are now missing from the list of persons in the supplement filed on 3/11/21. The Duties and Liabilities filed on 12/16/20 is incomplete at item 2 of the Acknowledgment of Receipt. A courtesy copy of the proof of service and the proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/26/21.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE AMENDED AND RESTATED STEPHEN N. COHEN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Reformation Of Trust Or, In The Alternative, For Modification Of Trust On Consent Of All Beneficiaries
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/27/2021. 1) The proof of service does not show notice was given to Luca Ravezzi. 2) Paragraph 25 of the petition must include Luca Ravezzi as a contingent beneficiary under Probate Code section 15804(a)(2) based on the disposition set forth in the first paragraph of Article 8.1 of the trust, though claimed to have been drafted in error. 3) Please provide a copy of the disclaimer. 4) No consent signed by Luca Ravezzi to the proposed trust modification pursuant to Probate Code section 15403 is on file.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE ELEANOR MCCLATCHY TRUST FOR THE PRIMARY BENEFI TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Of Co-Trustees For Settlement And Approval Of Trustees' Fourth Account And The Trustees' Acts Thereunder
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/11/21. As this is reportedly the fourth account but only the first before the Court, the co-trustees must file something to verify the values of the assets on hand at the beginning of this accounting period. Without such verification, the Court could only find and order that the account balances. Carolan Stiles is presumably a descendant of Sue Stiles, but the petition does not address this or whether there are any other (or more remote) issue of Sue Stiles. The list of trust beneficiaries also does not indicate under which remainder subtrust for a nephew listed in Article II of the trust agreement the named individuals would be beneficiaries. (Sue Stiles and other beneficiaries named in the trust agreement are presumed deceased?) There does not appear to be an order appointing a guardian ad litem for the minor remainder beneficiary. His age is also not set forth. Notice must be given directly to a minor who is at least 12 years old and also the person having legal custody of the minor, with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51(d) and Probate Code Section 1460.1.] This and the two related trusts are reportedly to be combined and distributed, but the Stipulation Staying All Proceedings Pending Appeal, etc., filed on 3/10/21 in case PTR-11-294985 (the "James Trust"), at paragraph 1 seems to refer to just that "Action" and not to this or the "Charles Trust" action. A courtesy copy of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/30/21.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAHUL BHIDE LIVING TRUST, TRUST CREATED AND EXECUTED NOVEMBER 13, 2012 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Ownership Of Trust Property And Authorize Conveyance
rulings.Rulings: continued to may 12, 2021 for proper notice. Judicial council form DE-115 has been a mandatory form of notice for 850 petitions since 1/1/20 and mailed notice is not sufficient for a person claiming an interest in the property (Mr. Jacob). see PC851(a).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HAROLD LEVINE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine amount of bond. Petitioner is a nonresident of California. Petition alleges $201,000.00 in personal property. Petitioner alleges there are no creditors.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JENNIFER S. CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: continued to 4/21/21. no response filed to the examiner notes sent to petitioner on 2/22/21. new deadline in order to avoid an appearance for the court to consider whether to allow the petition to remain on calendar is noon on 4/14/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSANNE CAPALBO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/11/21. (No earlier availability.) No proof of publication of the AMENDED petition has been filed yet. [CRC 7.53(a).] Notice mailed on 3/2/21 for a hearing on 3/8/21 was not sufficient notice. The person named in item 2b of the petition is missing from item 8 of the petition. Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. Item 6f of the petition should not be checked if a box above it is. Item 8 of the petition must clearly set forth how the parties are related to the decedent by identifying the ancestors through whom such parties take. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Whose children are the niece and nephew and when did that sibling of the decedent die? This Court requires the Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License form (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) to be filed separately and confidentially. None of the Waivers of Bond identifies the proposed personal representative. A courtesy copy of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/30/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NORMAN CLIFFORD SUMMERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/14/21. No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A.] If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Section 8113.] A courtesy copy of the proof of service and the proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/7/21.
